conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions. Let me start by understanding your situation:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - my son Alex. He's 18 years old. I'd like to know if I can still claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to understand his current situation, as the rules change once a child turns 16.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, is he in full-time non-advanced education such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or an approved training program?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any kind of education at the moment. He finished school and hasn't started college or anything like that. He's just figuring out what he wants to do next, really.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. I need to gather just a bit more information to complete the assessment.

**Does Alex live with you, and are you responsible for him?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me. I'm still responsible for him - he's under my roof, I feed him, do his laundry, all of that sort of thing.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing that information. Before I can make a final determination, let me read the official guidance to ensure I give you the correct answer.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for your patience. I've reviewed the official guidance and now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on my assessment of the official UK Child Benefit rules, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why: While you normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 16, the rules are different for children aged 16 or over. For children in this age group, you can only continue to receive Child Benefit if they are:
- Under 20 years old, AND
- In approved education or training (such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or approved training programs)

Since Alex is 18 and not currently in any education or training, he doesn't meet the requirements for Child Benefit, even though he lives with you and you're responsible for him.

**However**, if Alex decides to go back into approved education or training in the future (and he's still under 20), you could become eligible again at that point. If that happens, you would need to inform the Child Benefit Office about the change in his circumstances.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: According to the UK Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if they are under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Alex is 18 years old and is not currently in any education or training. Since he does not meet the requirement of being in approved education or training for children aged 16 or over, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex, even though he lives with you and you are responsible for him.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 18 years old. While Alex lives at home and the claimant is responsible for him, Alex is not in approved education or training. Under UK Child Benefit rules, for children aged 16 or over, they must be under 20 and in approved education or training to qualify. Since Alex does not meet this educational requirement, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for him.

For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if they are under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Alex is 18 years old and is not currently in any education or training. Since he does not meet the requirement of being in approved education or training for children aged 16 or over, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex, even though he lives with you and you are responsible for him.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 18 years old. While Alex lives at home and the claimant is responsible for him, Alex is not in approved education or training. Under UK Child Benefit rules, for children aged 16 or over, they must be under 20 and in approved education or training to qualify. Since Alex does not meet this educational requirement, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for him.
